channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tony Abbott Returns as Liberal Party President: Implications for Australian Politics
item.description: Former Australian Prime Minister Tony Abbott has re-emerged in national politics following his loss in the 2019 ‘teal’ wave. Abbott, who has remained a public figure since leaving parliament, has now ...

item.title: Congo expands Ebola testing as outbreak reaches 260 confirmed cases amid uncertainty over scale
item.description: Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo have expanded Ebola testing efforts as the number of confirmed cases has risen to 260, while experts warn that the full extent of the outbreak re...

item.title: Visa delays disrupt Bafana Bafana’s World Cup preparations ahead of Mexico camp
item.description: Bafana Bafana’s preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup have been thrown into disarray after administrative delays prevented the team from departing South Africa for their planned training camp in Pa...

item.title: Ralph Cu remains prepared after strong semifinal performances for Barangay Ginebra in the PBA
item.description: Barangay Ginebra guard Ralph Cu continues to keep himself prepared and focused after delivering solid performances during the semifinals of the PBA Season 50 Commissioner’s Cup. In the reported articl...

item.title: Gov. Nwifuru Appeals to Ebonyi Residents for Understanding and Support Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Governor of Ebonyi State, Francis Nwifuru, don appeal to him people make dem forgive any wahala wey dey for him government and those of the state lawmakers. He talk say no government fit perfect, ...

item.title: Mike Pence calls for abandonment of $1.8B U.S. settlement fund tied to Trump legal dispute
item.description: Former U.S. vice-president Mike Pence has publicly criticised a proposed $1.8-billion settlement fund created as part of a legal resolution involving former president Donald Trump and the Internal Rev...

item.title: Morgan Wallen overturns piano during Denver concert after apparent sound malfunction
item.description: Country music star Morgan Wallen experienced technical difficulties during a concert in Denver on 29 May 2026 while performing as part of his 'Still the Problem' tour. The incident occurred near the e...

item.title: Shooting near Watts vigil leaves one woman dead and another injured
item.description: Early Sunday morning, a shooting near a candlelight vigil in Watts resulted in one woman dead and another injured. According to the Los Angeles Police Department, officers responded to reports of gunf...

item.title: Essendon enter uncertain coaching transition under Dean Solomon amid ongoing leadership debate
item.description: Essendon Football Club has entered a turbulent new phase following the departure of senior coach Brad Scott, with Dean Solomon stepping in as interim coach amid significant uncertainty about the club’...

item.title: Over 200 injured and one dead after PSG Champions League celebrations in Paris
item.description: More than 200 people were injured and one person died in Paris following Paris Saint-Germain’s second consecutive UEFA Champions League victory, according to France’s interior ministry. The club secur...

item.title: Arusha students develop digital platform to improve waste management and recycling in Tanzania
item.description: A group of six Form Five students from Arusha Science Secondary School in Tanzania has developed an innovative digital platform called “Cyclo – turning waste into wealth,” aimed at improving waste man...

item.title: Nigeria to Boost Beekeeping and Livestock Development Under NL-GAS
item.description: The Minister of Livestock Development, Idi Mukhtar Maiha, don make am clear say the Federal Government dey committed to integrating apiculture, which na beekeeping development, into the National Lives...

item.title: Kerry-Lynne Findlay wins B.C. Conservative leadership amid party divisions and calls for unity
item.description: Kerry-Lynne Findlay has narrowly won the leadership of the British Columbia Conservative Party after a competitive and sometimes contentious multi-round ranked ballot contest. The race featured five p...

item.title: Uwaleke Urges Stronger Agricultural Support as Nigeria Faces Rising Food Insecurity
item.description: Professor Uche Uwaleke, wey be President of the Capital Market Academics of Nigeria, don raise serious alarm over the growing food crisis for Nigeria and call for urgent agricultural reforms. Him talk...

item.title: Police deploy additional personnel and traffic measures as schools reopen in Kochi
item.description: The police in Ernakulam district, Kochi, have strengthened their traffic management and safety arrangements ahead of the reopening of schools on June 1. Anticipating a significant surge in road traffi...

item.title: Two fatalities reported during violent protests in Mossel Bay, Western Cape
item.description: Two people have died following a series of violent protests that erupted in Mossel Bay in the Western Cape over the weekend. Police in the Southern Cape are currently investigating the circumstances s...

item.title: Arsenal celebrate first Premier League title in 22 years with city parade
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Arsenal supporters thronged north London's streets to celebrate the club's first Premier League title in 22 years. The players paraded the coveted trophy on open-top buses alo...

item.title: NBI clarifies subpoena on Nancy dela Rosa is not about locating Senator Bato
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) clarified that its plan to issue a subpoena to Nancy dela Rosa, wife of Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, is not intended to determine the senator’s whereabou...

item.title: A 97-Year-Old Letter From London, Ont., Reunites Author’s Descendants
item.description: In London, Ontario, a woman named Christina Archer discovered a 97-year-old handwritten letter while cleaning out her former home in 2001. The letter, dated August 13, 1929, was written by 15-year-old...

item.title: Waterloo Regional Police probe daytime residential break-in in Kitchener
item.description: Waterloo Regional Police are investigating a daytime break-and-enter at a residence in Kitchener, an incident that has raised concerns among local residents about neighbourhood safety during daylight ...
